public_title: Efficacy and safety of cisplatin plus radiotherapy versus standard care in locally advanced oral squamous cell carcinoma: a randomised controlled trial
scientific_title: Randomised controlled trial of concurrent cisplatin-based chemoradiotherapy versus radiotherapy alone in stage III/IV oral squamous cell carcinoma at tertiary cancer centres in India

intervention.name: Cisplatin 40 mg/m2 weekly + External beam radiotherapy 66 Gy in 33 fractions
intervention.details: Cisplatin administered IV weekly concurrently with definitive EBRT

comparator.name: External beam radiotherapy alone 66 Gy in 33 fractions

inclusion_criteria: Histologically confirmed oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC); Stage III or IVA (T1-T4a, N0-N2, M0) per AJCC 8th edition; Age 18 to 70 years; ECOG performance status 0 or 1; Adequate haematological function (Hb ≥10 g/dL, ANC ≥1500/μL, platelets ≥100,000/μL); Adequate renal function (serum creatinine ≤1.5× ULN or creatinine clearance ≥50 mL/min); No prior head and neck radiotherapy; Signed informed consent
exclusion_criteria: Prior chemotherapy or radiotherapy to head and neck region; Metastatic (M1) disease; Serious co-morbidities including uncontrolled diabetes (HbA1c >9%), active cardiac disease, uncontrolled hypertension; Pregnancy or lactation; Concurrent second primary malignancy; Hearing impairment requiring aid; Psychiatric illness precluding consent
study_design: Randomized, Parallel Group, Active Controlled Trial
